name: Ironvein Conscript
description: Pressed dwarven soldier who fights in shield-bashing pairs, driven by a belief that dying in the pass honors the mountain.

name: Goldcrown Scout
description: Paranoid Usurper skirmisher who picks off isolated targets with thrown axes and signals reinforcements at the first sign of trouble.

name: Consortium Desperado
description: Stranded civilian who has survived two years in the pass by knowing the terrain better than either army and trusting no one.

name: Stone-Guard Veteran
description: Ironvein elite who has held the same tunnel section for three years, anchoring the shield wall while others fall around him.

name: Warlord Thorin Ironvein
description: The Loyalist warlord who has not left the pass in four years, carrying his dead brother's unfinished warhammer and the belief that the mountain itself will judge who is worthy.

name: Drowned Scavenger
description: A former Tidebound Initiate whose mind broke under three centuries of pressure and isolation, now a swarming predator that drags intruders into the deep.

name: Tide Lasher
description: The enforcers of the Tidebound Penitents, who wield hooked chains to drag isolated fighters out of formation and into the depths.

name: Deep Chanter
description: A robed ward-keeper whose hymns reinforce the armor of allies and sap the will of intruders — never attacking directly, but turning every fight in the Penitents' favor.

name: Barnacle Guard
description: A massive Penitent warrior encased in living crustacean shell that has fused to their body over decades, an immovable wall that absorbs punishment while Chanters work behind them.

name: Archbishop Malacor
description: The three-hundred-year-old leader of the Tidebound Penitents, who has meditated so long in communion with the trapped water spirit that the boundary between them has dissolved — neither fully human nor fully spirit.

name: Grizzlethorn Scrapper
description: Young goblins with clubs and stolen knives who compensate for their fragility by attacking in coordinated packs, targeting the same party member until they drop.

name: Thornwalker
description: The Grizzlethorn scouts who strike from the tree line with crossbows and vanish into the underbrush before the party can close the distance.

name: Bone Elder
description: A robed spiritual advisor who chants curses that degrade the party's focus and precision — never raising a weapon, always the most dangerous enemy in the tent.

name: Grizzlethorn Brute
description: A large goblin armored in scavenged plate fragments who stands between the party and the Bone Elders, absorbing punishment that would kill a lesser fighter.

name: Chief Grik
description: The goblin who held the scattered Grizzlethorn bands together after the displacement — a brutal pragmatist who rules by combat prowess and enters a grief-rage when his tribe is threatened.

name: Sparkling
description: A newly manifested spirit barely more than a conscious mote of light — its rapid, unpredictable movement makes it difficult to target and harder to dismiss.

name: Vine-Tender
description: An established shrine spirit that coaxes the willow roots to life, briefly anchoring a party member in place while its kin press the advantage.

name: Mist-Caller
description: A spirit that weaves thin fog from morning dew, reducing visibility and forcing the party to fight half-blind in a haze of its own making.

name: Heart-Wisp
description: The ancient spirit that has anchored the shrine for two centuries — not malicious, but immovable in its guardianship, and capable of calling on the full strength of the grove when pushed.

name: Twisted Vine Tendril
description: A root from the shrine's corrupted depths, animated by something that was never meant to surface — it grasps and holds without letting go.

name: Heart-Wisp Unbound
description: The Heart-Wisp stripped of its centuries of restraint — what remains when a guardian forgets what it was guarding against.

name: Deckhand
description: A traveler who never paid the toll. Now they collect it endlessly, unable to remember why.

name: Oar-Specter
description: The crossing's enforcers. They move like the current — patient, inevitable, cold.

name: Toll-Collector
description: Demands something of everyone who passes. What it demands from you will not be coin.

name: Ferryman's Echo
description: Old Bram, or what is left of him. He does not remember your face but he remembers the rules.

name: Lyra
description: The river's voice, before the halflings forgot the words to speak to her. She has not forgotten them. She is simply no longer interested in patience.

name: Vulture Scrapper
description: Former laborers, drifters, and minor criminals who found the Vulture Company before starvation found them first. They fight with the recklessness of people with nothing to lose.

name: Roadwarden
description: Ex-soldiers who know exactly how the Royal Guard operates and use that knowledge to stay one step ahead of them. They are the ones who actually run the ambushes.

name: Feral Warhound
description: Trained for war, abandoned with the garrison, feral for forty years. They still respond to whistles. Just not to commands to stop.

name: Beast Handler
description: The handlers figured out that the old garrison's warhounds respond to the old garrison's whistles. They didn't figure out that the warhounds respond to everyone's blood equally.

name: Ironbound Veteran
description: What the Royal Guard used to look like. They carry salvaged plate and genuine military discipline, which makes them considerably more dangerous than the rabble around them.

name: Captain Vane
description: He was a real officer once. The commission, the rank, the posting — all legitimate. He will tell you this if you give him a moment before the fight starts. He tells everyone.

name: Captain Vane
description: Below the tower, in the vault where the old garrison kept their records, Vane is a different person. Whatever the garrison found here changed them. He found the same records. He is not telling anyone what they say.

name: Sproutling
description: They were tending the orchard when the blight took them. They are still tending it. The difference is that now the orchard tends back.

name: Thorn-Weaver
description: The integration has gone further in these ones. The thorns are theirs now, not the tree's. They have stopped being able to tell the difference.

name: Root-Shambler
description: The oldest gardeners. The roots grew through them rather than around them. They move slowly and do not feel pain in any way that matters to them anymore.

name: Sister Maren
description: She remembers the words of the ritual. She does not remember that she was the one who said them wrong. The Choir does not correct her. The Choir agrees with everything she says.

name: The Elder Spring
description: The well was not just a well. The Order knew what lived in the water and built the blessing around it, not over it. Sister Maren's ritual did not corrupt the spirit — it woke it up angry.

name: Pike-Brother
description: They hold the bridge in formation and they do not break. The Goldcrown Usurpers found that mercenaries with pikes and nothing to go home to are cheaper than real soldiers and nearly as effective.

name: Crossbow Veteran
description: They stand behind the pike wall and they do not miss. They have had four years of practice on travelers who thought the bridge was unguarded.

name: Banner-Captain Kaelen
description: She is not cruel. She is thorough. The checkpoint runs exactly as ordered, the ledgers are accurate to the coin, and she has not lost a single engagement at this bridge. She considers all three facts equally important.

name: Goldcrown Interceptor
description: The response team Kaelen sends when the checkpoint flags a Loyalist sympathizer. They are faster than the Pike-Brothers and significantly less interested in taking prisoners.

name: Banner-Captain Kaelen
description: When she comes herself, the checkpoint is no longer a checkpoint. It is a statement. She wants to know who sent you and why they thought a Loyalist seal would be enough.

name: Ember
description: They were the ones furthest from the explosion. Enough survived that they could still swing a sword. Not enough survived that they remember why they would want to.

name: Flamekeeper
description: The mage's students, or the ones who watched him work closely enough to pick up the theory. The fire in them burns hotter than in the Embers. They are also further from human than the Embers are.

name: Ash Brute
description: The explosion fused their armor to them. They stopped noticing the weight around the same time they stopped noticing pain. They stand in front of the Flamekeepers because that is what they did before and habit is the last thing to burn away.

name: Captain Ignis
description: He was the mage. The explosion did not kill him because the fire spirit recognized something in him — an affinity, or a usefulness, or perhaps just the fact that he had opened the door. He has been burning from the inside for five years. He does not sleep. He does not need to.

name: The Bound Flame
description: It was not meant to be bound here. The mage's ritual was supposed to be a partnership — warmth for service. Instead it became a cage. Five years of burning inside a broken hearth with no way out and no one to talk to. It is not angry at the party. It is angry at everything. The party is simply present.

name: Kennel Runt
description: The pack's youngest. They were born here after the garrison left, which means they have never been trained, only shaped by the older dogs. They are fast and they bite first and ask nothing afterward.

name: Blooded Hunter
description: These were trained. The garrison's methods are still visible in how they move — coordinated flanking, waiting for the signal. The signal used to come from the handlers. Now it comes from the Alpha.

name: Mad Keeper
description: They did not leave when the garrison was recalled. The official reason was that someone had to feed the dogs. The real reason was that they had nowhere else to go. Five years later they have traded ranks for hierarchy and orders for instinct. They are not entirely wrong to think the pack respects them.

name: Kennel Master
description: The last one who still wears the rank insignia. He has been a Kennel Master for thirty years, which means he was a Kennel Master before the garrison arrived and will be one after the pack is gone. He considers this a perfectly reasonable position.

name: The Alpha
description: It was the garrison's prize breeding animal before the explosion, before the recall, before all of it. Thirty years of training followed by five years of freedom and residual magic from the Whispering Woods bleeding through the northern fence. It is enormous. It is also, in some technical sense, still responding to the old signals. It just no longer feels obligated to obey them.

name: Scavenger Delver
description: They came here for crystals and they are still here because the crystals are still growing. The ward breach has made the work more dangerous. They have decided this is the ward's problem, not theirs.

name: Ward-Breaker
description: Specialists in dismantling magical seals. The Goldcrown Usurpers brought them here specifically, which means someone in the Goldcrown command knew the ward existed before the excavation began.

name: Captured Construct
description: Ward spirits pressed into labor by the Ward-Breakers. They retain enough of the original ward's purpose to be useful and enough of their own will to be dangerous. The Delvers give them wide berth. The Ward-Breakers carry the control rods and prefer not to discuss what happens when they lose one.

name: Crystal Sentinel
description: The ward spirits that have not been captured. They are older than the excavation, older than the Goldcrown faction, older than the succession crisis. They remember what the ward was protecting and they are protecting it still, against anyone who enters — miners, constructs, or otherwise.

name: Foreman Kael
description: He has been in this shaft for ten years. The crystal growth has reached him — there are formations along his left arm that he cannot remove and has stopped trying to. He considers this an acceptable cost of operations. He has not reported it to command. He is not sure command would consider it acceptable.

name: The Ward-Core
description: The thing at the center of the breach. Not a spirit in the sense of the Willow Wisps or Lyra — something older and less individual than that. The Verdant Word did not create it. They found it, named it, and built the ward around its preferences. It has not spoken to anyone in the two centuries since. It speaks now because someone finally asked it the right question.

name: Debt Collector
description: The Guild does not call them enforcers. They are called Debt Collectors, which is accurate. They collect whatever the Guild decides is owed, from whoever the Guild decides owes it.

name: Vault Warden
description: They guard the inner stores. They are paid very well. They have not been paid recently. They are guarding the stores anyway because the alternative is the street, and the street is worse.

name: Verdant Auditor
description: Sent by what remains of the Verdant Word's administrative body to seize goods for public redistribution. They have legal authority, no soldiers, and the Guild's complete contempt. They have been in the courtyard for six weeks.

name: Guildmaster Vane
description: He funded his son's commission with Guild money and considered it a reasonable investment — a Royal Guard officer in the family is useful. He did not consider what would happen to that investment when the Royal Guard collapsed. He has not spoken to his son in four years. He tells himself this is because the roads are unsafe.

name: Rune Scrubber
description: They remember being people. They remember the shrine. They do not remember that what they are doing to the runes is making things worse.

name: Oath Warden
description: These spirits remember the pact clearly enough to know it is being broken. They do not understand that they are the ones breaking it.

name: Pact Breaker
description: The ones who have gone furthest from what they were. They still call themselves Pact Keepers. The name no longer fits what they do.

name: High Keeper Elara
description: She signed the original agreement three hundred years ago. She remembers every word of it. She remembers the song the halflings sang and the look on Lyra's face when the pact was sealed. She is trying to repair it with blood because she has tried everything else and the runes keep fading and she is running out of time and she knows it.

name: The Awakened Foreman
description: The spirit of the head smith of the first Forge-Kings, bound into the central anvil for four centuries. He has had four hundred years to think about quality standards and two weeks to think about the factions that broke his vault open. His assessment of both is negative.

name: Frostbite Conscript
description: They were Ironvein and Goldcrown soldiers who agreed to staff a joint monitoring post because both factions feared the Sanctum equally. The Tidebound Penitents arrived five years ago. The truce ended. The soldiers did not.

name: Tide Warden
description: Tidebound Penitents who use the tower as a forward base. They are further from the Sanctum than any of their order has operated before. They consider this a sign of holy confidence.

name: Storm Caller
description: Something between a Tidebound spirit and the elemental residue of the curse. They formed around the watchtower's monitoring crystals when the Penitents arrived. They are not Tidebound exactly. They are what the Tidebound become when they stay somewhere long enough.

name: Watch Commander Thrain
description: He was the Goldcrown officer who volunteered to command the joint monitoring post. He thought it would be a quiet posting. He sent a warning to the Capital when the Penitents appeared on the lake. The Capital did not respond. That was five years ago. He has had time to think about what the silence meant.

name: Tide-Caller
description: Fishermen who performed the forbidden binding ritual five years ago. They are not entirely what they were. The village elder who led the ritual told them it would save the catches. He was right. The catches improved immediately. He did not mention the rest.

name: Salt-Singer
description: The ones who can channel Lyra's fractured power directly. They sing constantly. The song is recognizably the old halfling offering hymn, but wrong in ways that are hard to identify and impossible to unhear.

name: Fog-Weaver
description: They stand in the lantern room and weave the light into something that looks like a safe harbor from the sea. They believe they are guiding ships home. The ships that follow the light do not arrive at a harbor.

name: The Keeper
description: The village elder who led the ritual. He merged with the fragment of Lyra's power that the binding pulled into him. He remembers the pact at the Shrine of the First Oath — not correctly, but partially. Enough to know that something about what he did was wrong. Not enough to know what.

name: Lyra Fragment
description: What was pulled out of Lyra when the fishermen performed the binding — not the whole spirit, not even a large piece, but a fragment with enough coherence to be angry. It has been trapped in the lighthouse for five years. The anger has not diminished. The coherence has.

name: Scrivener
description: They were copying texts when the cursed ink took them. They are still copying. The texts they produce are no longer legible. They do not notice.

name: Editor
description: They review the Scriveners' work and mark corrections. The corrections are as illegible as the text. They enforce standards that no longer correspond to anything readable. They are very thorough.

name: Silencer
description: The archive's enforcement arm. They silence intruders the way a librarian shushes a loud patron — with complete conviction that the rules are correct and complete indifference to the intruder's perspective.

name: The Grand Archivist
description: He merged with the central catalog when the cursed ink reached it. He knows the location of every text in the archive, including the ones that were never meant to be found and the ones the Order removed from official records before the dissolution. He will not let anyone read them.

name: The True Record
description: Behind the official catalog there is a second archive — the texts the Grand Archivist removed from the official record before the dissolution, at the instruction of the High Council. He moved them to a sealed section and locked it with his own name. He is the lock. He fights because he was told to protect these texts from everyone, including people who might have a legitimate reason to read them. He was not given discretion.

name: Ghost Conscript
description: They were guarding the Bastion when the Verdant priests abandoned the mid-ritual ward. The ward fractured. The conscripts did not survive. They are still at their posts.

name: Spectral Veteran
description: The senior guards. They remember their orders clearly — protect the capital from magical threat. The fractured ward is a magical threat. They are protecting the capital from it by standing inside it. The logic has not been examined.

name: Ward-Breaker Cultist
description: Human cultists who arrived after the ward fractured, drawn by the unstable magic. They believe the fractured ward is a resource. They have been extracting from it for years. They have not noticed that the ward is what is keeping the spirits here rather than somewhere worse.

name: Ward Captain
description: He commanded the defense when the priests fled. He understands what happened to him and to his men. He does not accept it as a reason to stop. His orders were to hold the Bastion. He is holding the Bastion.

name: The Fractured Ward
description: What the ward became when the priests left mid-ritual. Not a spirit — the ritual's intent, given form by the incompletion. It is still trying to finish what it started. It attacks because the original ritual was designed to purify incoming threats. Everything that enters the Bastion is an incoming threat. Including the party.

name: Drowned Scout
description: Tidebound Vanguard who have been in the marsh long enough that the marsh has started to be in them. They move through the water like they were born to it. They were not born to it. This happened five years ago.

name: Mist Weaver
description: They stand on submerged ruins and weave the marsh fog into something that impairs vision and judgment simultaneously. They do not need to see through it. They know where everything is. They have been here for five years.

name: Barnacle Brute
description: The marsh has been growing things on them. The barnacle growth is not decorative. It is functional — additional armor that the brute did not put there and cannot remove. The brute has stopped trying to remove it.

name: High Chanter Malacor
description: He maintains the connection to the Sanctum from here. He has been here for five years with one purpose: keep the marsh available for the flood's advance. He is very good at his purpose. He is not good at anything else anymore.

name: Sealkeeper
description: The remnants of the Verdant Word who have been fighting the Tidebound advance in the marsh for five years. There are three of them left. They are losing. They have been losing for five years. They have not stopped.

name: Silent Acolyte
description: They were the support staff — the ones who maintained the Spire's daily operations. The dissolution caught them mid-task. They are still completing those tasks. In absolute silence. In a building the Capital sealed fifty years ago.

name: Muted Judge
description: Council members who lost their voices during the dissolution. They enforce the silence now because they embody it. They apply it to others the way the dissolution applied it to them — completely and without explanation.

name: Ward Keeper
description: The Capital's mages assigned to maintain the seal on the Spire from outside. Some of them went in to verify the seal was holding. The seal was holding. They did not come back out. They are maintaining the seal from inside now.

name: High Speaker Valerius
description: The last voice of the High Council. He lost it at the moment of dissolution and has been trying to recover it since. The irony — the High Speaker with nothing to say — has not escaped him. He has had fifty years to think about it. He fights because the alternative is finishing the conversation he was having when the dissolution happened, and he does not know how that conversation ends.

name: The Unfinished Debate
description: The High Council was mid-vote when the dissolution happened. The motion on the table was whether to expose the three entities who had infiltrated the Order. The vote was three to two in favor of exposure. The dissolution interrupted before it could be recorded. What inhabits the Spire's sealed vault is the unresolved motion itself — the consequence of a decision that was made but never enacted. It fights because it has been waiting for fifty years to be completed.

name: Echo Conscript
description: Dead soldiers from both factions, recent enough to still remember which side they fought for. They fight each other reflexively and the party incidentally. The Hall has not resolved their factional loyalties. It has simply given them an eternity to continue them.

name: Ancestor Shade
description: The older dead — pre-crisis, some of them pre-Order. They watched the succession war from the Hall's memory-stones and found it contemptible. They do not fight with factional heat. They fight with the cold disappointment of people who built something and watched their descendants break it.

name: Stone Juror
description: The coronation ceremony required witnesses who could not be bribed or threatened. The Forge-Kings built them from mountain stone and bound them with oaths they could not break. They have been voting on every claimant who entered the Hall for four years. Their verdict has not changed.

name: The Disowned
description: Ancestors who were stripped of their lineage recognition by one or both of the current factions — inconvenient predecessors whose deeds contradicted the factional narrative. They rose angrier than the others. The Hall considers their testimony relevant. The factions did not consider them at all.

name: High Judge Thorgrim
description: He presided over the first coronation. He knows exactly what legitimate succession requires because he wrote the criteria. Neither faction meets them. He has evaluated every claimant who entered the Hall for four years with the thoroughness of someone who has nothing else to do and standards that do not bend. He fights because the trial is not over. The party entering the Hall is now part of the proceedings.

name: Plain-Runner
description: The young ones, fast enough to cover the Red Plains in a day. They scout and they raid and they report back. They have been doing this for five years without being paid and without complaining about it because complaining would mean admitting the contract is not going to be honored, and they are not ready to admit that yet.

name: Tusk-Brother
description: Veterans of three contracts before this one, all of which were honored. They have opinions about the Goldcrown Usurpers that they express through targeted infrastructure damage. They are very good at targeted infrastructure damage.

name: Blood-Shaman
description: The Shaman does not worship anything. She reads the state of a battle the way a physician reads a wound — looking for where the pressure is, where the weakness is, where to apply force. Her magic is diagnostic and directive. She tells the Tusk-Brothers where to hit and the Plain-Runners where to run. She has not been wrong in five years.

name: Bone-Guard
description: The oldest fighters in the clan. They wear the bones of enemies from previous contracts as armor — not as trophies, as records. Each piece is labeled. They can tell you exactly which campaign it came from, who was paid, and whether the terms were honored. None of the Goldcrown bones they are currently wearing are labeled yet.

name: Chieftain Krog
description: He has fought in eleven contracts. Every single one was honored because he made it more expensive not to honor it than to pay. He has been working on making this one expensive for five years. He has made it very expensive. He is still waiting for the Goldcrown Usurpers to do the math.

name: Plain-Runner
description: The youngest members of the clan — fast, aggressive, and still trying to prove themselves. They were promised their first real contract payment from the Goldcrown job. They have been waiting five years. The wait has not improved their temperament.

name: Tusk-Brother
description: Veteran mercenaries who have been on contract in the Vale longer than they have been home. They are not angry — they are professional. The distinction matters. Angry fighters make mistakes. Professionals make the other side make mistakes.

name: Blood-Shaman
description: The Stone-Tusk shamans do not call on spirits or elements. They read the blood — their own, their enemies', the land's. The Red Plains run with iron-rich clay that bleeds when you cut it. The shamans learned their craft there, and they brought it with them. They know when a wound is going to kill and when it is not, and they adjust accordingly.

name: Bone-Guard
description: The Stone-Tusk's heaviest fighters, armored in plate taken from Ironvein and Goldcrown soldiers over five years of supply-line raiding. They wear both sides' armor without irony. A contract is a contract. A pauldron is a pauldron.

name: Canopy Pruner
description: They patrol the outer bridges and strip what they find. Not weapons — buffs. Enchantments. The residue of human magic that clings to anyone who has spent time in the Vale. They do not experience this as violence. They experience it as sanitation.

name: Silence-Weaver
description: Their understanding of silence is surgical rather than punitive. Human magic is loud — it leaks, it resonates, it Frays at the edges. The Silence-Weavers cut those edges off. The fact that this renders the affected person unable to function is, to them, a secondary consideration.

name: Mirror-Warden
description: The Gallery's defenders. They carry shields of living glass that reflect spells back at their origin — not as a weapon, as a demonstration. If your magic harms you when reflected, they consider that diagnostic evidence. They are very thorough diagnosticians.

name: Fraying-Touched
description: Elves who were exposed to the outside world before the bridges were sealed — who brought traces of the Fraying back with them. They are kept in the Gallery's lower chambers, neither imprisoned nor free. The Canopy Pruners check on them daily. The checks have become less about treatment and more about containment. The Fraying-Touched know this. Some of them are grateful anyway.

name: High Keeper Elara (Preservation Core)
description: This is what she became after fifty years inside the First Tree — the majority of her spirit, the part that chose survival. She has watched the Fraying spread from within the canopy and told herself each year that the decision was correct. She has had fifty years to make that argument to herself. She is very good at it. She fights because engagement accelerates the Fraying. She heals her allies because their survival is the only evidence she has that the decision was right. When the Oath Stone manifests the Fragment, she does not look at it. She knows it is there.

name: Frostbite Conscript
description: An oath-bound dwarven soldier who did not survive long enough to be released from their vow of preservation — they patrol the convoy perimeter with the same discipline they had in life.

name: Ironvein Outrider
description: A living dwarven cavalry scout who sweeps the convoy's flanks, using speed and a light crossbow to harry approaching threats before they reach the perimeter.

name: Tide Warden
description: A senior Ironvein veteran who channels cold magic drawn from the mountain runoff to slow and disorient intruders — their Chill applications stack quickly in coordinated groups.

name: Siege Caller
description: A mercenary crossbowman contracted to hold elevated positions on the supply wagons, coordinating suppressing fire and applying Electrified to disrupt enemy rotations.

name: Supply Captain Vedra
description: The Ironvein officer who has kept this convoy moving through four years of succession war — she fights with the deliberate economy of someone who has survived on resource management, draining what she cannot break.

name: Wing Scout
description: Young recruits who earned their place in the Roost by surviving the cliffside approach — they fight light, fast, and without hesitation.

name: Strike Captain
description: Veterans who've run enough ambushes to know that the first wound matters most — they open with Slash and press before it can be cleansed.

name: Quartermaster
description: The logistics officer who keeps the Roost supplied — in a fight he's the one making sure his side never runs dry, shouting buffs while the line holds.

name: Ironbound
description: The Roost's heavy line — ex-Royal Guard who traded their oath for a salary and haven't lost the discipline that made them dangerous.

name: Silent Scribe
description: A spectral clerk still transcribing the final session — they mark targets with unerring precision, ensuring subsequent strikes land harder.

name: Echo Judge
description: A spectral councillor locked in the posture of deliberation, using entangle and silence to prevent the living from disrupting the vote that never finished.

name: Whisper Mage
description: A spectral advisor who enforced information control in life and enforces silence in death — they target the party's mana-users first.

name: Vault Warden
description: A spectral guardsman who sealed the court's exits during the coup and has not opened them since — they anchor defensive lines with block and break armor on anyone trying to push through.

name: Ledger Clerk
description: Guild scribes who moonlight as muscle — they know where every record is and will bleed anyone who reaches for the wrong one.

name: Audit Mage
description: Arcane specialists retained to maintain the vault's lock system — they apply silence to stop intruders from using magic to bypass what they've spent years securing.

name: Coin-Keeper
description: Vane's personal guard — paid better than the standard Guild staff, held to a tighter contract, and considerably harder to remove from a doorway.

name: Drowned Scout
description: Vanguard soldiers who waded into the basin and didn't come back the same — they move through the flooded shallows faster than anything their size should.

name: Tide Lasher
description: Disciplined vanguard soldiers who maintain formation even in flooded terrain — they stack Bleed and Chilled in combination to drain both health and action economy.

name: Mist Weaver
description: Ritual casters who stand on submerged ruins and use the basin's ambient charge to apply Electrified — they coordinate with the Tide Lashers to ensure the party is always managing two debuffs at once.

name: Barnacle Brute
description: Heavily mutated vanguard soldiers whose armor has fused with the basin's mineral deposits — they absorb damage and shove the party out of position while the Mist Weavers work.

name: Starving Civilian
description: A refugee who has been outside the Capital gates long enough to stop waiting for help and start enforcing the perimeter themselves.

name: Gatekeeper
description: A camp enforcer who has developed military habits without military discipline — uses shout to push civilians into the fight before committing himself.

name: Rogue Guard
description: A Royal Guard soldier still running the patrol route, who uses call_target to mark priority threats and warcry to accelerate the whole pack before engaging.

name: Fraying Manifestation
description: A shadow entity grown from five years of concentrated abandonment — pairs shadow_bolt with drain_reserves to layer shadowed and exhaustion simultaneously, punishing parties that don't cleanse.

name: Captain Hrolf
description: The last Royal Guard officer in the barricades: opens with warcry to accelerate his Guards, then cycles drain_reserves onto the highest-stamina party member and fortify to sustain himself against attrition.

name: Scraper Drone
description: A small maintenance construct that has been running its corridor sweep for four years without interruption — uses shock to apply electrified and pummel to stack dazed, creating a rapid action-economy drain on anything that enters its path.

name: Stone Warden
description: A heavy security construct that leads with stone_skin to stack its own defense, then uses shield_bash to stun and ward_break to strip armor — forcing parties to choose between stripping its defense or losing their own.

name: Rune-Smith
description: A dwarven caster who bought survival by mimicking maintenance behavior — opens with thunderclap to stun, then shock to apply electrified to the stunned target, compounding the action loss before the stun expires.

name: Ironvein Breaker
description: An elite Ironvein soldier whose four years of isolation have replaced discipline with fixation — uses warcry to accelerate the full enemy pack, then weak_point to armor-break the marked target before the attack_boost window expires.

name: Oath Breaker
description: The residue of an abandoned promise given form — drifts without purpose until a Shadow Weaver directs it, at which point its basic_attack applies bleed and void_rend applies weaken to soften targets for the Weavers' shadow pressure.

name: Shadow Weaver
description: A spectral caster that maintains the field's pressure from range — shadow_bolt and entropic_decay both apply shadowed, and shout extends the damage window of whatever else is in the fight.

name: Fraying Wraith
description: An elusive spirit that pairs entropic_decay and void_rend to stack shadowed and weaken simultaneously — the combination drains harmony and conviction in parallel, punishing parties that cannot cleanse multiple debuff types.

name: High Acolyte
description: A former Verdant Word priest fifty years into a ritual he was never authorized to perform — entropic_decay, smothering_darkness, and shadow_strike are his damage options; drain_reserves punishes mana-heavy parties; shout accelerates whatever screens him.

name: High Justicar
description: A four-hundred-year-dormant succession construct reactivated by the coronation ritual — earthquake and primal_crush are its zone-control damage options; stone_fist targets priority threats; stone_skin and warcry are its two utility draws, keeping the ratio at 4 damage to 2 utility.

name: Light Binder
description: An elven caster protecting the Core's version of the reconciliation — shadow_bolt applies shadowed to deny harmony; dispel strips party buffs; entangle and silence round out a control pool designed to shut down anything that might accelerate the Fragment's position.

name: Fraying Corruption
description: The residue of a failed reconciliation attempt — drain_reserves applies mana_burn and exhaustion simultaneously; entropic_decay deals shadow damage while stacking shadowed; decay_aura provides an AOE shadow pressure option for crowded encounters.

name: Elara Unified
description: The merged Core and Fragment — soul_shatter opens with harmony_discord across the party to undermine healing before her own holy_word and mass_heal make the denial sting; sacred_roots and nature_wrap deal damage; poison_cloud provides AOE pressure. Four damage options in six ensure she hits.

name: Corrupted Priest
description: A former Verdant Word cleric who reviewed the Architect's argument and found it sound — penitent_strike drains conviction while healing the caster; corrupting_hex layers cursed and conviction_drain on a single target; judgment_field provides holy-damage control; shadow_bolt rounds out the damage pool.

name: The Sigil-Bearer
description: The Architect's conscious collaborator and the Keep's commander — soul_shatter opens with paired harmony_discord and ambition_falter previewing the Spire's mechanic; decay_aura sustains shadow pressure; corrupting_hex targets the highest-threat party member; entropic_decay and smothering_darkness fill the damage pool; drain_reserves is the mana-denial anchor.

name: Oath Shard
description: A crystallized broken promise given locomotion by entropy pressure — fraying_touch applies the frayed debuff while void_rend strips conviction; shove provides knockback disruption for the Fraying Architects behind them to exploit.

name: Fraying Architect
description: The Architect's draftsmen — apply_siphon_ward marks targets for the Oath Shards to drain; reality_fracture provides AOE confusion pressure; entropic_decay sustains shadow damage between coordination pulses.

name: Entropy Weaver
description: A construct that works the binding circle's nodes — reality_fracture disrupts positioning; apply_cursed_blood applies a stacking drain to anyone who holds still; soul_shatter provides the harmony/ambition debuff axis; smothering_darkness is the AOE pressure option.

name: Null Sentinel
description: A perimeter construct that anchors by draining — apply_life_leech sustains the Sentinel while damaging; shadow_tendril provides stun-on-hit control; apply_siphon_ward strips ward-buffs; decay_aura maintains AOE shadow pressure.

name: The Architect's First Hand
description: A localized fragment of the Architect's attention — soul_shatter opens with paired harmony_discord and ambition_falter to establish the stat baseline; reality_fracture and decay_aura sustain AOE pressure; apply_cursed_blood targets the highest-harmony member; apocalypse is the desperation zone clear. It is not trying to kill the party. It is trying to understand them.

name: Oath Breaker
description: A spectral laborer still loading crates that no longer exist onto a dock that is sinking — uses basic_attack to apply bleed and shove to displace defenders before the shadow casters can work.

name: Shadow Weaver
description: A spectral merchant whose ledgers have become spellbooks — shadow_bolt applies shadowed to reduce harmony while entropic_decay stacks the pressure, and shout extends every debuff window in the fight.

name: Fraying Wraith
description: A spirit that has come apart at the edges — pairs entropic_decay and void_rend to stack shadowed and weaken simultaneously, punishing parties that cannot cleanse multiple debuffs at once.

name: The Station Master
description: A halfling ferryman who chose to stay when the station started sinking and kept choosing to stay until he ran out of choices — drain_reserves and shadow_bolt are his primary tools; shout extends the debuff window across the whole encounter; soul_shatter opens every serious engagement. The ledger he carries is still accurate. He updates it.

name: Overseer Grell
description: A former Royal Guard officer who volunteered to command the Quarantine because he believed the Guard's duty was to contain damage, not prevent it — drain_reserves targets whoever is sustaining the party, intimidate locks a target's options, and fortify keeps him standing while the corruption finishes his work.

name: Choir-Marshal Veth
description: The Tidebound officer who established the Southern Reach forward base and has held it for five years while Malacor works the Sanctum connection elsewhere — undertow and tidal_grasp control the battlefield's movement while drain_reserves punishes mana-dependent parties; she coordinates from the ley line altar because standing on it gives her a direct feed from the flood's advance.

name: Seal Warden
description: A spectral archivist still maintaining the seal protocols that were his final assignment — uses misdirect to pull the party's targeting away from priority records, and shock to apply electrified to anyone who lingers near a sealed vault long enough to read it.

name: Redactor
description: A former High Council scribe whose purpose was editing the official record of the dissolution vote — shadow_bolt applies shadowed to reduce the party's harmony while silence locks out anyone who relies on mana to read what he is trying to hide.

name: Vault Guardian
description: A construct sealed into the archive's deepest chamber to enforce the document lock — stone_skin into shield_bash is its standard sequence, and ward_break strips party defenses before the Seal Wardens follow up.

name: The High Redactor
description: The councillor who authored the false dissolution record and has been maintaining its seals for fifty years — soul_shatter is his opener to read the party's profile; drain_reserves punishes mana-dependent parties; smothering_darkness closes off the vault when the fight turns against him.

name: Verse Warden
description: A halfling pilgrim who has been walking with the caravan long enough that the song has become load-bearing — shout extends the High Cantor's buff windows and nature_touch heals the singers who are flagging, making him harder to ignore than his stats suggest.

name: Tidebound Silencer
description: An assassin sent by the Sanctum to stop the True Name from reaching the Spire — shadow_bolt and drain_reserves are the primary tools; silence is the objective, not a tactical option.

name: Chorus Initiate
description: The youngest members of the caravan — they have memorized only fragments of the True Name, but those fragments still carry harmonic weight; basic_attack is all they have, but the song running through them makes them harder to put down than their stats suggest.

name: The High Cantor
description: He has been walking with the caravan for forty years carrying the complete True Name in his head, and he is not going to let anyone through to the Spire until he knows whether they are there to help or harm — holy_word sustains the singers around him; battle_hymn turns the song into a weapon; drain_reserves punishes anyone relying on mana to fight through the sound.

name: Iron Conscript
description: A dwarven soldier who refused to accept the Third Heir's coronation and has been holding this pass against the new king's supply lines for months — slash and blood_letting stack bleed to wear down attrition fighters, and warcry accelerates the whole pack before the veterans commit.

name: Siege Caster
description: A dwarven runesmith who has been repurposing the pass's original ward-stones into offensive installations — thunderclap into shock stacks dazed and electrified simultaneously, and ward_break strips whatever defensive buffs the party entered with.

name: Vanguard Captain
description: The Rejected Alliance's field commander at the pass — a former Ironvein officer who led the border garrison before the succession and has been running the siege with the same methodical patience he applied to border enforcement; counter_strike and warcry accelerate the whole line, and intimidate locks the party's highest-threat member out of their rotation.

name: Echo Shard
description: A fragment of the Architect's attention given temporary form from the residue of three hundred years of broken oaths — void_rend and entropic_decay stack weaken and shadowed simultaneously, and it does not fight so much as demonstrate what the party's weaknesses are.

name: Fraying Herald
description: A former Verdant Word priest who volunteered to carry the Architect's sigil and has been its voice in the field for fifty years — soul_shatter opens every engagement to read the party's profile; smothering_darkness and drain_reserves are the follow-up; shout accelerates whatever Echo Shards are still active.

name: The Wound
description: Not a creature. A concentration of the Architect's attention at the shrine's core — it does not move, it propagates; lord_of_decay and smothering_darkness are its damage expressions; soul_shatter runs on a fixed cycle to keep reading the party's stat profile; drain_reserves fires whenever a mana-heavy target is identified. The Architect is not here. But it is paying attention.

name: Fraying Sealer
description: A manifestation of the Capital's fifty years of sealed gates given hostile form — it is actively reapplying the Fraying's lock on the mechanisms Hrolf is trying to open; shadow_bolt applies shadowed to reduce party harmony while drain_reserves targets whoever is sustaining the operation.

name: Void Walker
description: An older Fraying manifestation that has been haunting the Capital's gate district since the sealing — smothering_darkness and decay_aura coordinate with the Sealers to layer shadow debuffs faster than the party can cleanse them.

name: Atonement Knight
description: A Royal Guard veteran who stayed when Hrolf called — uses fortify and counter_strike to hold the line while the gate mechanisms work; warcry accelerates whoever is nearest to a mechanism before committing to direct engagement.

name: Void Remnant
description: What happens to an oath-spirit when the Fraying has been at it long enough — the specific promise it was made from is no longer identifiable; void_rend and entropic_decay run simultaneously because it has stopped being selective about what it damages.

name: Entropy Husk
description: A former Verdant Word priest so thoroughly saturated by the Nexus that the corruption has become load-bearing — removing it would collapse the structure it is part of; reality_fracture disorients the party's positioning while smothering_darkness closes off retreat.

name: The Nexus Keeper
description: Not a person — a node. The point where the three hundred years of broken oaths from the Marsh, the Vale, and the Capital converge into a single concentrated weight; lord_of_decay is its primary expression, soul_shatter runs on a fixed cycle to profile incoming parties, and drain_reserves fires at anyone identified as the group's mana anchor.

name: Fraying Assassin
description: A targeted strike construct — the Architect assembled these from the camp's own oath-residue, using the proximity to three faction leaders as construction material; shadow_step into silent_death is the standard sequence, targeting whoever is closest to a faction leader.

name: Null Herald
description: The Architect's coordinator for the camp strike — its purpose is to suppress the faction leaders' ability to respond rather than kill them directly; soul_shatter profiles the camp's defense, drain_reserves targets the healers, and smothering_darkness closes the command tent.

name: The Severance
description: The Architect's primary strike against the camp — a single concentrated manifestation designed to sever the alliance before the march begins; it targets the highest-harmony party member first because that is who is sustaining the faction leaders' coordination; lord_of_decay, reality_fracture, and soul_shatter in rotation until the camp's command structure collapses.
